evidence_excerpt: 10 months after programming, aided thresholds with the cochlear implant were 35–40 dBA; he can discriminate environmental sounds and react properly in daily life, but still needs visual clues for verbal communication and his speech production improves at a slower rate.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Ongoing hearing/communication limitations persist despite improvement: he still needs visual clues for verbal communication; speech production improves at a slower rate.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: During surgery, a profuse CSF gusher was encountered; it resolved after head elevation within 15 minutes.
Adenoidectomy was performed preceding cochlear implantation due to adenoid hypertrophy and recurrent otitis media.
10 months after programming, aided thresholds were 35–40 dBA; he can discriminate environmental sounds and react properly to sounds in daily life.
He has 50–60 meaningful words, has begun to use two-word combinations, and can monitor his own voice.

notes_on_uncertainty: The record reports two pediatric cases. Extraction focuses on Case 2 (5-year-old boy) because more detailed mid-term outcomes (10 months) were documented. Long-term (>12 months) outcomes are not available; the overall outcome reflects the latest documented status rather than true long-term follow-up.
